data.title: TEAM plc transforms into international wealth platform with WH Ireland and EPIC deals
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

TEAM plc ( (GB:TEAM) ) has provided an update.

TEAM plc, the wealth, asset management and complementary financial services group, reported strong organic growth for the year to 30 September 2025, with revenues up 16% to £12.0m and client assets rising 11% to £1.29bn. All three divisions contributed, led by its International arm, while Investment Management expanded via model portfolio services and new UCITS funds and Advisory grew as more clients moved into discretionary mandates, helping narrow adjusted EBITDA losses as operational leverage improved.

Since the year end, TEAM has executed a step-change in scale by completing the all-share acquisition of UK wealth manager WH Ireland and announcing two EPIC transactions, collectively adding around £1bn of assets under management. These deals establish a meaningful FCA-regulated UK presence, lift group AUM to more than £2.3bn, broaden the client base and infrastructure, and are intended to accelerate growth by shifting the mix toward higher-quality recurring revenues and tighter integration of advice and investment management across UK and international markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:TEAM) stock is a Hold with a £26.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on TEAM plc stock, see the GB:TEAM Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TEAM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TEAM is a Neutral.

TEAM plc’s overall stock score is primarily impacted by its financial performance and technical analysis. The company’s strong revenue growth is overshadowed by profitability and cash flow challenges. Technical indicators suggest bearish momentum, and valuation metrics are unfavorable due to ongoing losses.

To see Spark’s full report on TEAM stock, click here.

More about TEAM plc

TEAM plc is a wealth and asset management group providing discretionary investment management, model and bespoke portfolios, fund management, advisory and international financial services. The company focuses on International Finance Centres and key regions including Jersey, Guernsey, the UAE, Malaysia, Singapore and South Africa, serving wealthy individuals, trusts, institutions and international clients through its Investment Management, Advisory and International divisions.

DCC plc ( (GB:DCC) ) has issued an update.

DCC plc has reported that, as of 31 March 2026, it has 87,609,229 issued ordinary shares of €0.25 each, of which 2,185,094 are held in treasury and do not carry voting rights. This leaves 85,424,135 ordinary shares in issue with voting rights, a key reference figure for investors monitoring their holdings under U.K. disclosure and transparency rules.

The updated share capital and voting rights structure is primarily relevant for regulatory reporting and compliance purposes under the FCA’s Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules. Shareholders must now use the 85,424,135 voting shares as the denominator when assessing whether changes in their positions trigger notification obligations, ensuring continued clarity over ownership thresholds and market transparency.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:DCC) stock is a Buy with a £4984.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on DCC plc stock, see the GB:DCC Stock Forecast page.

data.title: BlackRock Elevates Jay Jacobs to Lead Bitcoin Trust
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

IShares Bitcoin Trust Registered ( (IBIT) ) has provided an update.

On March 27, 2026, Jay Jacobs, a Managing Director at BlackRock and U.S. Head of Equity ETFs since April 7, 2025, was appointed to the board of iShares Delaware Trust Sponsor LLC and named its President and Chief Executive Officer, placing a seasoned ETF strategist at the helm of the sponsor of the iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF. Jacobs brings extensive experience from BlackRock, Global X ETFs, and the New York Stock Exchange, while on the same date former President and CEO Shannon Ghia resigned from the sponsor and its board, with the company stating her departure did not stem from any disagreement over the trust’s operations or policies.

The leadership change underscores BlackRock’s intention to align the bitcoin trust sponsor more closely with its core ETF expertise and strategic direction, potentially reinforcing product development and client engagement in the fast-growing crypto-related ETF segment. Stakeholders in the iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF may view the appointment as strengthening governance and strategic oversight, given Jacobs’ background in equity and thematic ETFs and the confirmation of an orderly, non-contentious transition from Ghia’s tenure.

More about IShares Bitcoin Trust Registered

iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF is a digital asset investment vehicle sponsored by iShares Delaware Trust Sponsor LLC, an affiliate within the BlackRock group that focuses on exchange-traded fund products. The trust provides investors with exposure to bitcoin through a regulated ETF structure, aligning with BlackRock’s broader strategy in index and thematic exchange-traded funds.

Average Trading Volume: 68,151,086

Current Market Cap: $53.76B

See more data about IBIT stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Chagee Posts Lower 2025 Profit as Restructuring and Overseas Expansion Weigh on Margins
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Chagee Holdings Limited Unsponsored ADR ( (CHA) ) is now available.

On March 31, 2026, Shanghai-based Chagee reported unaudited results for the fourth quarter and full year ended Dec. 31, 2025, highlighting a teahouse network of 7,453 locations, up 15.7% year over year. Total 2025 GMV rose 7.2% to RMB31.58 billion, but Greater China GMV fell in the fourth quarter while overseas GMV jumped 84.6%, reflecting shifting demand and faster international growth.

Fourth-quarter 2025 net revenue declined to RMB2.97 billion and the company swung to an operating loss of RMB35.5 million due to restructuring, a business model transition and higher share-based compensation. For 2025 as a whole, net revenue grew 4.0% to RMB12.91 billion, but operating margin roughly halved to 10.4% and GAAP net income dropped to RMB1.19 billion, as Chagee boosted investment in company-owned and overseas stores, corporate infrastructure and organizational changes that pressured short-term profitability.

The most recent analyst rating on (CHA) stock is a Hold with a $11.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Chagee Holdings Limited Unsponsored ADR stock, see the CHA Stock Forecast page.

The company’s attractive P/E ratio and high dividend yield are significant positives, but recent declines in revenue and profitability, along with bearish market momentum, suggest caution. data.title: Galapagos Strikes New Gilead Deal to Co-Develop Gamgertamig and Unlock $500 Million for Strategic Moves
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Galapagos ( (GLPG) ) has shared an update.

On March 31, 2026, Galapagos announced a binding framework agreement with Gilead Sciences tied to Gilead’s planned $1.675 billion acquisition of US biotech Ouro Medicines, securing shared rights to Ouro’s T cell engager portfolio for autoimmune diseases. The deal centers on gamgertamig (OM336), a clinical-stage BCMAxCD3 T cell engager with Fast Track and Orphan Drug designations in the U.S. for autoimmune hemolytic anemia and immune thrombocytopenia and promising early Phase 1/2 data, with registrational studies expected to start as early as 2027.

Under the agreement, Galapagos will fund 50% of the Ouro acquisition consideration and milestone payments and will co-fund development of gamgertamig, bearing all pre-registrational costs and sharing registration-enabling expenses equally with Gilead. Gilead will take global commercialization responsibility outside KeyMed’s territories, paying Galapagos tiered royalties of 20–23% on net sales and potential milestones, while Galapagos also gains three additional preclinical autoimmune programs on which Gilead may later opt into 50/50 profit splits.

A key feature is an amendment to Galapagos and Gilead’s prior collaboration, allowing Galapagos to deploy at least $500 million of its cash independently of Gilead, including up to $150 million for possible share buybacks or other capital returns, which enhances strategic flexibility and capital allocation options. The transaction, treated as a related-party deal given Gilead’s 25.35% stake in Galapagos, was reviewed under Belgian corporate law, with an independent board committee and the statutory auditor both concluding that the terms are not manifestly disadvantageous to Galapagos and are justified by the expected benefits.

The most recent analyst rating on (GLPG) stock is a Buy with a $32.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Galapagos stock, see the GLPG Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GLPG Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GLPG is a Neutral.

GLPG scores strongest on financial positioning (very low leverage and large cash resources) and supported valuation (low P/E). The main offset is persistent negative operating/free cash flow and earnings quality concerns highlighted by non-recurring profit drivers and ongoing restructuring/wind-down cash needs; technical signals are broadly neutral.

To see Spark’s full report on GLPG stock, click here.

More about Galapagos

Galapagos NV is a Belgium-based biopharmaceutical company listed on Euronext and Nasdaq, focused on developing innovative therapies, particularly in immunology and autoimmune diseases. The company partners with large pharmaceutical groups such as Gilead Sciences to co-develop and commercialize advanced biologics and cell-engager programs targeting severe, immune-mediated conditions.

Average Trading Volume: 133,932

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $1.92B

data.title: Unilever Spins Off Foods in $44.8bn McCormick Tie-Up to Sharpen HPC Focus
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Unilever ( (GB:ULVR) ) has issued an announcement.

Unilever has agreed to combine its Foods business with McCormick & Company, creating a $20 billion-revenue global flavour group spanning herbs, spices, seasonings, cooking aids, sauces and condiments. The combined business, which will retain the McCormick name and leadership while adding senior Unilever Foods executives, will own iconic brands such as McCormick, Knorr and Hellmann’s and is targeting about $600 million in annual cost synergies within three years.

The deal values Unilever Foods at $44.8 billion and will see Unilever and its shareholders receive 65% of the combined company’s equity plus $15.7 billion in cash, while McCormick shareholders will hold 35%. The transaction, expected to close by mid-2027 subject to regulatory and shareholder approvals, will refocus Unilever as a pureplay home and personal care group with a higher structural growth profile, stronger premium and emerging-market exposure, and capital returned to investors via debt reduction and a planned €6 billion share buyback.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:ULVR) stock is a Buy with a £5062.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Unilever stock, see the GB:ULVR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ULVR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ULVR is a Outperform.

The score is driven mainly by steady profitability and reliable free cash flow, tempered by higher leverage. Technicals are supportive (price above key averages, positive MACD) but appear overextended on RSI/Stoch. Valuation is somewhat demanding at ~22.7x earnings, while the ~3.44% dividend yield and earnings-call outlook (cautious growth guidance plus buyback and cash generation) provide support.

To see Spark’s full report on ULVR stock, click here.

More about Unilever

Unilever is a global consumer goods group with a major presence in beauty, wellbeing, personal care and home care. The company focuses on high-performing, innovative brands across fast-growing categories and geographies, with significant exposure to emerging markets and digital commerce channels.

Central Asia Metals ( (GB:CAML) ) has issued an update.

Central Asia Metals has confirmed that as of 31 March 2026 its issued share capital stands at 177,904,281 ordinary shares of US$0.01 each, of which 193,325 shares are held in treasury. This leaves a total of 177,710,956 voting rights, a key reference figure for investors monitoring their disclosure thresholds under the UK Financial Conduct Authority’s transparency rules.

The announcement provides clarity for shareholders and market participants on the company’s current capital and voting structure, which is important for regulatory reporting and for assessing ownership changes in the AIM-quoted miner. By updating the market on its voting rights, Central Asia Metals supports compliance with disclosure requirements and maintains transparency around its shareholder base and corporate governance framework.
